feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Process Automation
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Process Automation Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: BPI_FORMS_RUNTIME_SERVER_ERROR: Unable to each runtime server
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently working on a Business Process project using SAP Build Process Automation, and I’ve encountered a runtime server issue when trying to import the task UI of the workflow. The error message displayed is:

 

Process followed : 

1. Verify the HTML5 Applications in BTP :

2. Import your SAP UI5 form in SAP Build Process Automation



3. Filled the Import Form details:

 

Issue  : 





What I’ve Tried

  • Verified that all services in SAP BTP Cockpit are up and running.

  • Checked for any missing form configurations or deployment issues.

  • Tried redeploying the process and re-authenticating with my BTP account.

What I Need Help With

  • What exactly causes the BPI_FORMS_RUNTIME_SERVER_ERROR?

  • Are there specific services or destinations that must be enabled for the runtime server to be reachable?

  • Has anyone faced similar issues when working with SAP Build Process Automation forms or workflows?


    Environment Details

    • SAP BTP: Cloud Foundry environment

    • Service: SAP Build Process Automation

    • Use Case: Custom workflow with Task UI used for approvals

 

 

If anyone has faced and resolved this issue or has any insights your help would be greatly appreciated. Please feel free to share possible causes or diagnostic steps to try.

Thanks in advance!

 

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Actions in SAP Build Process Automation -- Need to call $Batch in Action Task
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am trying to implement a batch call in Build Process Automation using an Action Task. I have configured the destination in the BTP Cockpit and also created an Action Project in SAP Build.

When testing the Action Project independently, I am able to retrieve the expected data. However, when using it within a workflow, I am encountering the following error:

Action Identifier:
post_batch
Destination:
GET_DATA
Error Message:
Action '7a1f2496-6c0d-48ce-a724-9ca2651d8fab' failed with the following error:
'Action metadata not found in database for tenant f5a191b7-809f-4113-87fe-6b21da19d542, project us10.93112af8trial.getdatacall, version 1.0.3, action group b315b59d-bafd-3cbf-a4a2-79a0743dfac0, and action post_batch'.

Log ID:
fdc268b4-2c5c-439a-814b-802b67e-us10

I have also attached a screenshot of the configuration.
Please let me know how I can resolve this issue.

Destination Config Screenshot:

Action Config Screenshot:

 

BPA workflow Screenshot:

Thanks,

Parth

entry.title: If I run the automation in test mode of BPA, will it be counted as 1 connection and charge the cost?
entry.content:
Hi, 

I am testing the scenario as following in the sap build process automation.

1. Retrieve the file content of the uploaded attachment file in the DMS.

2. Convert the file content to a specific file type and download in local path.

3. Send the downloaded file to the outlook mail.

 

I created the automation for the above processes.

I didn't release and deploy the entire project yet.

And now I would like to run in test mode.

If I run by using test mode, the desktop agent will be opened and the package will be loaded to desktop agent.

So, I would like to know that if it will be counted as 1 connection for attended (or) unattended mode and will charge a cost for it?

If not, when the connection will be counted to charge a cost and how many connection will be needed for my scenario?

Could you please explain for me?

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: Am I Prepared for the C_LCNC_2406 Exam? Looking for Final Tips!
entry.content:
Hi everyone!

I'm getting ready to take the C_LCNC_2406 – SAP Certified Associate - Low-Code/No-Code Developer exam and would really appreciate some input from those who've been through it.

Here's what my prep looks like so far:

  • Completed BTP100 & BTP110 (Citizen Development fundamentals)
  • Finished APP400, SPA400, and WZ400 courses
  • Gained hands-on experience with SAP Build Apps, Process Automation, and Work Zone
  • Scored around 70–75% on mock tests
  • Using P2PCerts practice questions, which have been helpful for understanding the format and question style

Based on your experience, does this sound like solid prep?
Are there any key topics I should review again or any additional resources you’d recommend?

Thanks in advance for any tips or insights — especially from those who’ve passed recently!

entry.title: Build Process Automation get workflow instances from shared environment
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm trying to get the workflow instances that are running in a shared environment in build process automation from Postman.

If I try to get them from the public environment I get a list of the running instances, but from the shared environment I get a 200 OK with an empty list. 

I put the environmentId as query parameter and irpa-api-key as header and configured the authorization. I'm able to activate a process in a shared environment from Postman, so I know those settings are correct.

I also made sure that there are running instances in the shared environment.

Does anyone know where the problem lies?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Error "Rule Service with given ID does not exist" when Consuming Decision Table API(SBPA)
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have created a decision table in SAP Build Process Automation and have successfully released and deployed the project. Following this, I created a trigger from the deployed decision table.

However, when I try to consume this API via Postman, I encounter the following error message:

"The rule service with given id xxxxxxxxxxxxx does not exist."

Has anyone come across this error before? Any insights or suggestions to resolve this would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Call a destination(OAuth2UserTokenExchange) from SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
I am trying to call a destination from SAP Build Process Automation.

The destination is configured with authorization OAuth2UserTokenExchange.

I get error while using the "Call web service with destination" activity and also while configuring the actions.

Please look at the attached screenshots.

Kindly help me on how to resolve this.

Regards

entry.title: Help with Role Type and Business Partner Fields in 4JA Mass Maintenance Template
entry.content:
I'm currently working on the Mass Maintenance of Enterprise Projects (4JA) scenario in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This process is part of the SAP Build Process Automation content package, which enables us to perform bulk updates to enterprise project master data using an Excel-based input template uploaded via SAP Build Work Zone.

While preparing the input template for upload, I’m unclear about the correct values to provide for the following fields: 

a) Role Type

b) BP or BP GUID number

 Regards,

Gunadharshini

entry.title: How to Generate a Dynamic URL for Approval Task in SAP Business Process Automation (SAP BPA)
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I'm working on a workflow using SAP Build Process Automation and need help with generating a dynamic task approval link that can be sent via email notification to the approver.

Objective:

  • Generate a dynamic URL for each task instance.

  • Send the URL via email template or script task.

  • Clicking the link should open the approval task directly in My Inbox or SAP Build Work Zone / Launchpad.

Thanks in advance,
Parshuram

entry.title: User does not have sufficient privileges in Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi

I have created a process automation like a bellow example 
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-academy-salesorder.html

here it is working fine, and workflow is triggering while creating the instance from Process and Workflow>> Start New instance and Close. 

But when i created the Build App for the API like bellow:

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-workflow-trigger.html

But here it is giving error at the time for workflow instance 

"responseText": "{\"error\":{\"code\":\"bpm.workflowruntime.rest.user.not.sufficient.privileges\",\"message\":\"User does not have sufficient privileges.\"}}"

I have assign the role also, But still giving error. Can any one help me please....

entry.title: SAP Build Action: Error fetching BTP subaccount destinations for subscriber tenant context.
entry.content:
I'm trying to test an Action in SAP Build. However, when trying to select a Destination, I get the the following error:
 
Error fetching BTP subaccount destinations for subscriber tenant context.
This error could happen for the following reasons:
Correlation ID: xxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
 
This is happening for all Actions.

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: Why is SAP Desktop Agent required for all automations?
entry.content:
I'm looking at putting together a SAP Build Process Automation project using only cloud services, so no local file storage or Excel / Outlook SDK.  In other words I'm looking to remove the SAP Desktop Agent dependancy.

However, on running the main process, the automaton is never called because it appears SAP Desktop Agent is a prerequisite for any automation, regardless of the dependencies (I'm using the CORE SDK only).

Can anyone confirm the above? Or is there a way of setting up an SAP Build Process Automation without the need for SAP Desktop Agent?

entry.title: Difference in behavior of action call when called in debug mode vs unattended mode?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing the following issue on one particular action call.

Issue details:

1. Testing the action call directly on the action project works and provides the expected result.

2. Added the same action project as a dependency in an automation. Calling the action call via action groups. When executing this action group call in debug mode, provides the expected result.

3. Only when running the automation in unattended mode, the action call provides an unexpected result.

Already checked the following:

1. oData call on gateway server is working correctly.

2. Latest version is used for both manual and automated testing.

3. Same destination is used in debug and unattended mode.

4. Same data tested in both cases.

I am unsure of the direction I should next take to identify the issue.

Any direction or pointers would be much appreciated.

Best Regards,

Rohit

entry.title: Anyone Successfully Using SAP Desktop Agent on Mac?
entry.content: Can anyone confirm if the SAP Desktop Agent is available for Mac and whether it works properly? I remember there were some issues with installing it on Mac earlier. If anyone is currently using it on a Mac, could you please share your experience—specifically, whether it's functioning smoothly or not?

entry.title: Facing issue while deploying a UI5 application
entry.content:
Integrate an SAP UI5 Task in a Business Process | SAP Tutorials 
I was following the above blog for integrating UI5 and automation. Well, I have built an automation process and a UI5 application as well, but the deployed application is not showing up in the HTML5 applications tab as shown below in the images:

 



 

Despite after giving many tries, it fails to show the deployed app link. Can I get any assistance regarding this issue, please ?

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: Can an automation be ran without the SAP Desktop Agent?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently working an SAP Build Process Automation pilot project and have reached a point where the team is ready to start looking at deployment options in terms of how the automation will be ran. It seems like the desktop agent is necessary to run a scheduled unattended trigger. Ultimately, we want to avoid having to setup a virtual machine to carry out this particular automation. The below image contains our automation process and the options we see available. What would be the best way to run this particular scenario without the Desktop Agent? The automation currently uses the Outlook SDK, but research has led us to find that if we use Microsoft 365 SDK instead, we can run this scenario without the agent. Are these findings valid? If not, I would appreciate feedback on the best approach to help us move forward!

 

 

Regards,

Patrick Pienkowski

entry.title: SAP Process Automation: Simplifying Business Workflow
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced digital environment, businesses are constantly looking for ways to work smarter and faster. That’s where SAP Process Automation comes in. Whether you're working in finance, HR, procurement, or supply chain, SAP Process Automation can help you get rid of repetitive tasks and streamline your operations. But what exactly is it, and why should you care?

Let’s break it down.

 What is SAP Process Automation?

SAP Process Automation is a solution that allows organizations to automate business processes without writing complex code. It combines the power of workflow management and robotic process automation (RPA) into a single, easy-to-use platform.

Put simply, it helps you:

  • Design business workflows
  • Automate repetitive tasks (like data entry)
  • Reduce manual errors

Free up employees to focus on higher-value work

 Key Features

Here are some of the most useful capabilities of SAP Process Automation:

1. Drag-and-Drop Workflow Builder

Non-technical users can design business processes visually using a simple interface—no coding needed.

2. Bots (RPA)

Software bots can mimic human actions like clicking buttons, filling forms, or copying data from one app to another.

3. Pre-Built Content

SAP offers a library of pre-built workflows and bots for common business scenarios like invoice processing, employee onboarding, or purchase order creation.

4. Integration with SAP and Non-SAP Systems

The tool works seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA and can also interact with external systems like Excel, email, or web portals.

 Real-World Use Cases

Here are a few examples where SAP Process Automation shines:

  •  Finance: Automating vendor invoice approvals and payments
  • HR: Handling employee leave approvals or document generation
  •  Procurement: Automating purchase requisitions and purchase order processing
  • IT: Auto-resolving simple support tickets or resetting passwords

 Benefits of SAP Process Automation

Benefit Description

  • Saves Time Replaces manual, repetitive tasks with automated flows
  •  Reduces Errors Ensures data accuracy and compliance
  •  Cost-Efficient Saves costs by reducing the need for human intervention
  • Scalable Easily adapts as business needs grow
  • Secure Enterprise-grade security and access controls

Getting Started

To begin using SAP Process Automation, you typically need:

  • An SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) account
  • Access to the SAP Process Automation service
  • Basic understanding of your business process workflows
  • Training or help from SAP documentation or partners

 SAP Process Automation vs Traditional Automation

  • Aspect SAP Process Automation Traditional RPA
  • Integration with SAP Deep integration May need connectors
  • Ease of Use Low-code/no-code Usually requires scripting
  • Intelligence Built-in AI/ML features Limited without add-ons
  • Scalability Cloud-native and scalable Depends on setup

Conclusion

SAP Process Automation is a game-changer for companies running on SAP. It brings together the power of workflow design and robotic automation to simplify complex operations and boost productivity.

If you're tired of chasing approvals, updating spreadsheets, or entering the same data in five different places, it might be time to explore automation.

entry.title: Using javascript normalize("NFD") in IRPA/BPA
entry.content:
Hi, I wonder why its not possible to use normalize("NFD") directly in drag and drop acitvities in BPA?

for example something like "stepX.text.normalize("NFD")" - such formula gives error

But its possible to create custom script and use it the and it works.

Why is it like that? 

Thank you.

entry.title: Error while launching the recorder (NO_RESPONSE)
entry.content:
I have this error when trying to use Recorder instead of Manual Capture. But it happens only when I want to add new screens to current application. When using Recorder for first time, it works. But any time later, it gives me this error and I have to use manual capture. I tried with multiple tenants with no luck. Thanks for any suggestions.

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: SAP Build Apps/Process Automation: "Workflow Definition Not Found" Post Shared Environment Redeploy
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm encountering a frustrating issue with SAP Build Process Automation and its integration with SAP Build Apps, and I'm hoping someone here might have some insights.

I initially deployed my process to the Public Environment and was able to successfully trigger it from an SAP Build App without any problems. However, due to the need to enable notifications, I had to switch to a Shared Environment. After redeploying the same process to this new shared environment, the integration with SAP Build Apps stopped working.

I'm now consistently receiving the following error message when trying to trigger the workflow from my SAP Build App:

 

{"error":{"code":"bpm.workflowruntime.rest.definition.not.found","message":"Workflow definition not found","logId":"xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx"}}

This clearly indicates that the SAP Build App can no longer locate the workflow definition in the new environment.

Here's what I've already tried to resolve the issue, without success:

  1. Deployed the process into a brand new project in the Shared Environment.
  2. Verified the deployment status in the Library within SAP Build Process Automation to ensure the process is indeed deployed and active in the Shared Environment.
  3. Reconfigured the integration within the SAP Build App by updating the process and action references to point to the newly deployed process in the Shared Environment.
  4. Created a completely new action within the SAP Build App, linking it to the redeployed process.

Despite these steps, the "Workflow definition not found" error persists. It seems like the SAP Build App is still looking for the old definition in the Public Environment, or perhaps there's a caching issue, or a deeper configuration problem with Shared Environments that I'm overlooking.

Has anyone encountered a similar problem when migrating processes from a Public to a Shared Environment, or when dealing with "Workflow definition not found" errors after a redeployment? Any suggestions or troubleshooting steps would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance for your help.
